Name: dynamic_moe_kernel_optimization
Title: Dynamic MoE Kernel Optimization via Hardware-Aware Profiling and Fused Kernel Techniques
Short Hypothesis: By employing fine-grained hardware profiling and targeted kernel fusion techniques, we can significantly reduce the inference latency of Mixture-of-Experts architectures while maintaining accuracy across diverse workloads.
Related Work: Existing works such as AdaFuse and KernelFoundry explore dynamic MoE structures and genetic optimization for kernel generation, but they often lack the rigorous hardware profiling that isolates performance bottlenecks. Our proposal emphasizes empirical, trace-based optimization specific to the GPU architecture, aiming to provide reproducible performance gains that are directly attributable to hardware characteristics.
Abstract: Dynamic Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures have become central to state-of-the-art performance in large language models. However, their inference often incurs significant latency penalties due to irregular memory access patterns and expert routing overhead. This proposal introduces a novel approach to optimize MoE performance through targeted kernel fusion and hardware-aware profiling. We will instrument and profile the execution of MoE models using real GPU hardware to understand the latency contributions of each component, focusing on memory bandwidth utilization and compute occupancy. Building on these insights, we will develop a fused kernel that combines the routing and computation steps into a single pass, reducing the overhead from multiple CUDA kernel launches. We will empirically evaluate our approach on multiple NVIDIA GPUs, measuring p50/p99 latency and throughput under various input conditions. Our work aims to bridge the gap between theoretical model architectures and practical implementation, yielding reproducible, optimized kernels for deployment.
Experiments: 1. Profile the current MoE implementation on NVIDIA GPUs using nvprof/Nsight to identify latency bottlenecks.
2. Develop a fused CUDA kernel that combines routing and computation for the MoE architecture and compare it against the baseline in terms of latency and throughput.
3. Conduct A/B testing on varying batch sizes to evaluate the impact of the optimized kernel under different load conditions, tracking metrics such as p50/p99 latency and energy per token.
Risk Factors and Limitations: Potential risks include the complexity of accurately profiling dynamic workloads and the challenge of ensuring that the fused kernel maintains accuracy across different input distributions. Additionally, the degree of performance gain may vary significantly based on the specific GPU architecture used, limiting generalizability.
